name: Fave Dolci
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Jo Merrill Chewy cookies from southern Italy that are served on All Souls' Day. The custom stems from the ancient tradition of offering beans to Pluto and Proserpina, god and goddess of the underworld in Roman mythology.
1. Grind almonds finely in a food processor using on/off pulses until they resemble a fine powder.
2. Add sugar and blend until powdery.
3. Add butter cut into 1/2-inch pieces, egg, lemon peel, orange flower water, and cinnamon; process to a paste.
4. Mix in flour just until incorporated, using several on/off pulses. The dough will be sticky.
5. Transfer dough to a bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate overnight.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. Grease and flour baking sheets.
8. Roll rounded teaspoons of dough into short, thick cylinders.
9. Arrange the cylinders on prepared sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
10. Using your finger or the handle of a wooden spoon, press an indentation in the side of each cookie to form a lima bean shape.
11. Flatten slightly with your hands.
12. Bake until cookies are just beginning to color, about 16 minutes.
13. Cool on baking sheets for 5 minutes before transferring to racks and cooling completely.
14. Store in airtight containers.
